name: Abbott, Helen
motto: A smile will go a long, long way.
description: Helen is the girl with the smile — ask the boy friend! She's not the quiet kind — ask her roommates! This, however, has nothing to do with the fact that she is a member of the Glee Club. Her ability to obtain "special permission" to attend movies in Dover is the envy of us all. Perhaps her marks account for this. Lucky girl, would that we were all as bright!

name: Abbott, Harold Elliott
description: In "Hot-shot" Abbott, the radio announcer of WEEI, lies the future of Chemistry as a science. Why a man from the north always goes south toward Methuen for a week-end is still a puzzle. "Abbie" maintains that it is immaterial whether one is right or wrong, an argument is an argument.

name: Abrahamson, Herman Oscar
description: "Abe" came north from the "Land of Mosquitoes," and settled in Durham. Despite the fact that he is a handsome blond, he has developed into an able pugilist under the coaching of Sergeant Brown. Now he swings a wicked pillow, but he is seldom seen in Smith or Congreve. He is always smiling and nothing ever can vanquish that grin from his rosy countenance.

name: Africa, Isabel
description: "Izzy" transferred to U. N. H. from Lasell to continue her studies in home economics, but so far as we can see, she hasn't yet had much time to devote to her chosen course of study. She is out on Saturday night with one man, and Sunday afternoon finds her entertaining another, for "Izzy" knows how to keep 'em guessing — (we hear she had some experience in high school.) We are sorry that she didn't join our class sooner, so that more of us might have an opportunity to know her better.

name: Ahern, Daniel Keleher
motto: I like to go fast
description: That is the "Speed Demon's" favorite expression. If you ever saw him drive the fire-engine, you'd believe us. In his spare time, besides athletics, janitor-work, fireman, and social activities, he attends classes quite successfully. He is one of our two-fisted wildcats - if you don't think so, try to stay in the ring with him for a couple of rounds.

name: Aiken, Oscar Sumner
description: "Ocie" is a great hunter, but for some reason all his hunting seems to be done in Farmington. What he hunts, no one knows, but we all have our suspicions. "Ocie" is also a noted mathematician of the Liberal Arts college, and is now engaged in adding 3 and 2.

name: Allyn, Marjorie Elizabeth
motto: Oh, the world is wide, the world is grand, And there is little or nothing new, But the sweetest thing is the grip of the hand, Of a friend that is tried and true.
description: That is "Midge" - "a friend that is tried and true." Her ready smile and cheerful "Hello" make her a popular girl. She transferred from Lasell Seminary this year and is taking the Home Economics course, a matter for which we, who are invited to her "spreads" are thankful to the gods.

name: Appleton, Scott Severance
description: 7-3-5-36-11 shoot. These are not football signals. They merely denote "Scottie" using a slip-stick. If the man were a fish he would be a shark, at least where math is concerned. "Some day," "Scottie" says "I shall be chief gun-elevator in the Army of Occupation," and that is not all that is elevating about our "Scottie" either.

name: Armitage, William O.
description: "Bill" is a quiet, unassuming fellow, and but few know of his real value to his class. He aspires to be an architect, and consequently spends the greater part of his time in the drafting room. Judging from the posters and other works of art that "Bill" has produced, he is sure to be a success in his chosen profession. The co-eds fail to worry "Bill." He believes that distance lends enchantment, for the "one and only" is forty miles away, and he receives his letter every day.

name: Ashe, Harry Burbank
description: Harry went out for cross-country his Freshman year. We think he dropped out because he intends to become a Chemical Engineer. We don't see much of Harry week-ends, because the fact is that he isn't here. We all envy the lucky girl in Hampton. He may surprise us all some day, for he sure does shoot a mean show.

name: Atkins, Reginald French
description: "Reggie," or "Racetrack," came to us from the Capitol City with a sweater that was so loud that it kept the campus awake at night. He had visions of becoming a chicken magnate and so took all the poultry courses he could. He is a mean speedster on the track, breaking the quarter-mile record during his Freshman year. He is thinking of going into the ice business when he graduates, but he is afraid he might get the cold shoulder.

name: Auerbach, Eugene K.
description: From the Library to Demeritt in a half hour flat. "But I will break my own record yet," says "Pooch," who is only a shell of his former self after a strenuous sophomore year at B. U. "Inertia" has returned to us this time to stay, and any day you can see him dragging his weary body over the campus at a snail-like pace, and although it is said that he starts out a day ahead in order to be on time, he has been known to make his classes in the same day.

name: Avery, Carroll Wood
motto: Ask 'Buck' which is the most beautiful town in New Hampshire. — Wolfeboro, of course.
description: Carroll is a student, first and foremost. Witness his Delta Chi and Phi Lambda Phi pins. Aside from being a student, he has that indescribable elusive something which Elinor Glyn calls "It." If not, why the daily letters from Medford, and the absence of his fraternity pin, which he claims is being fixed. We wonder. You're there, "Buck."

name: Balch, Grant Pushee
description: "G. P." hails from the north country, but no one would ever suspect it because he is so quiet. He keeps out of sight most of the time so we can't tell much of his history, but we understand that he is a horseman and is quite a breeder of cats.

name: Baldi, Atilia Mary
motto: Tillie the Toiler
description: But our "Tillie" doesn't have to toil, everything she does just happens without excess work. It happens she can naturally play soccer with vim, shoot baskets with alertness, successfully cook cakes and pies, receive 90's in courses, dance gracefully, hit a baseball with accuracy, and lob a tennis ball. What more could you expect from a girl who is full of pep and has a smile like "Tillie's"?

name: Barron, Katherine Frances
description: "Kay" works in a post office, receiving and sending out mail — but that's not the only line she is busy with mails. Males occupy a good part of her time going to and from classes, trains, etc. "Kay" is a good kid though and makes herself known to all, "Lest" she be forgotten when she leaves.

name: Bartlett, William Stuart
motto: Bill is one of the most handsome men on the campus, and the co-eds gaze longingly at him as he passes them on the way to class. However, Bill gives the women no time and spends long hours on his engineering work. We are told that over week-ends he spends the greater part of his time in the famous game of Midnight Poker. Bill is really quite a student, as he received his Ph.D. (Pool Hall Duke) degree some time ago. Everyone feels sure there is a place for him at Hollywood.

name: Barton, Philip Shaw
motto: Some day Phil hopes to hang up his M. D. shingle, but just at present he divides his time between Smith Hall and Animal Husbandry. He is also making a strong bid for a certain captaincy and we don't mean in athletics either. We have also heard a rumor about a pin that has been lost and found. He claims Cumberland as his home town, but his main attraction is in Durham.

name: Batchelder, Edna Gertrude
motto: Very meek and quiet - yes, 'till you really know her! She's a good sport and a pal to all. Just ask her to do a favor and see if she refuses! She is a sure cure for the blues. Industriousness personified - try to find her idle a moment - if it isn't Physics or Math., it may be something more frivolous.

name: Batchelder, Helen Agnes
motto: We fear that Helen's Saturday trips to Lee would almost get the best of her if it weren't for the frequent letters from Hooksett. Oh yes, she's a good kid, even though she is a home wrecker.

name: Batchelder, Ray Merton
motto: Look High
description: One of the Local Boys. "Batch," like the rest of his family, is an industrious personage and always has a smile. When not engaged in the classroom he is busy trucking. He is a great admirer of people who can handle horses and train German Police Dogs.

name: Bean, Willard Frederick
description: His mother calls him Willard, but the fellows call him "Jelly," and on rare occasions when hungry for mirth, they call him "Lima." He is red hot and that's no static. The cold climate of his native country has not cooled off his latent ardor for the weaker sex. In fact, the reason why he studies mathematics is because he is interested in "figures," but for all his faults and failings, "Lima" is a good boy to work and we hope that his first million comes easy.

name: Beede, Frank Ellsworth
description: Ever since "Doc" Bowler asked "How is everything at Pow-wow River this morning?" Frank has meandered up and down the campus wearing a broad grin. He has the easiest job at the Marshall House, and is one of the most inspiring officers of the R. O. T. C. Without doubt "Beed" is the most illustrious manager the Freshman Cross-Country Team ever had. He spent so much time in Boston with the team that there was some doubt of his passing E. E. "Beed" has claimed every term that he was due to flunk out, but he generally pulls through with an average of 85.

name: Beggs, Martin Francis
description: "Mart" came to New Hampshire with the class of '27. When the winter winds blew Marty blew too. Hot cakes in Texas once a day and sleep one night a week is not all it's cracked up to be and a welcome blew him back. He's dodging minds forever, so he says.

name: Woodbury Dow Bell
motto: Jingle
description: Bell hails from the little town of Hollis where they grow apples that are apples, what I mean. He has slung hash at the Commons for three years. He is one of the very few fellows on the campus who drives a car around without paying for the gas and not incurring the wrath of the Dean. He sure is lucky. One of his favorite pastimes is taking Botany trips. He never comes back with the flowers but—? Here's hoping he is as successful raising apples as he is raising the devil. If he is, he sure will be a success in the tough battle of life.

name: Paul Parker Bickford
motto: Bick
description: Dean Pettee's favorite tornado while whirling through the wilds of Chocorua picked up this handsome lad and deposited him in front of the Dairy Building in 1924. He has been blowing ever since as far south as Boston and as far north as Plymouth—Normal School, with occasional stops at Concord. Although some people believe he hasn't the right attitude, we think he is more than equal for most of them. Just ask him about the dance in Detroit, or the functions of the inner ear.

name: Harold Francis Birmingham
description: To this good companion—our friend, our pal, The class of '28 knows only too well That in him we have a true blue friend Whose helping hand will always extend To those who troubles have to bear; And in our happier moments will he also share.

name: Roland Lester Bissonett
motto: Bizz
description: "Bizz" came down from Claremont and won his way to fame via the air route. When it comes to jamming air into a horn and producing melody, there's none can compare with this "Doo-Wacka-Doo" kid. It is rumored that he has a way with women, and his fraternity brothers would tell you that it's more than a rumor when trips to Boston occupy such a prominent part of his schedule.

name: Blaisdell, Margaret Ester
description: Basketball games are "Peg's" specialty especially when she knows who is to be the referee. Dances also seem to have a kind of fascination for her — she just can't keep away from them. But then, why should she? For, oh, boy! how she can dance!

name: Blodgett, Grace Burnham
motto: Number Please?!
description: Grace may spend some of her time popping this question, but how about the rest of her time? Since her leave of absence from Congreve, Gracie has made her headquarters nearer Smith Park, a delightful place. Well, Gracie's chief hobby is horseback riding, even though she doesn't always stick to the saddle.

name: Bond, Jerry Jr.
motto: Say, listen Uff.
description: Debating is Jerry's specialty. If you feel like arguing, go and see Jerry, and lose all your feeling. By possessing this ability, he has permanently become Mr. Ufford's right hand man. At least, he is his mainstay on the debating team. We cannot say very much about Jerry's love affairs, as he keeps them pretty much to himself. However, his smile will go a long, long way if he ever does get started.

name: Boodey, Leon Eli
motto: A
description: He is another hero of the Marshall House — he has cut his dinner course there only twice in three years. It is rumored that for such a quiet fellow he spends more time in Dover than any other man on the Campus. There are some, however, who dispute this. Among them is Proctor Barnes of the second floor of Fairchild. He claims "Bood" makes more noise than any other four men in the dormitory. Some of the occupants wish it were true, as the old place "ain't what it used to be!"

name: Bradley, Charles Stuart
motto: The best is yet to come.
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Finance Committee (3)
description: Everyone who knows this big, blushing, bashful boy falls in love with his winning ways. He is a dangerous competitor of Johnny Dolan, the world's premier cornetist, having "tooted" his horn in the Band for two years.

name: Brannen, Mildred Evelyn
motto: The best is yet to come.
description: Curly hair? Yes! Friendly grin? You bet! Always ready for a good time? Well, I should say! Of course I know her - that's Evelyn Brannen. It's said that she is qualifying for varsity track by her daily sprints from Bagdad Road to "T" Hall. She sure has the pep and isn't she the one who gets the birthday cakes?

name: Bridge, George Samuel
motto: The best is yet to come.
description: When "Chuck" came down from Hebron, we thought he would miss the slopes of Maine, but he soon found that New Hampshire had a "little field," and pines not for his native state. This big, handsome brute thrills the fair sex with his spectacular shots from any angle of the basketball court, but very few are favored with more than a smile or a nod. George's name will, no doubt, be seen in many advertisements for beauty preparations and hair curlers.

name: Brooks, Lester Stewart
motto: The best is yet to come.
description: Say, "Brooksie," how's your lumbago? Debate or rebate? "Les" is a square and honest man. He loves deeply and truly, and soon it will be the chimes for "Brooksie." He made a botch of the job of Business Manager of the GRANITE, but the book came out just the same. If he keeps on the way he is going, he will be President some day - maybe. He will hate to be separated from "Joe" and "Frankie," but he should worry, he will still have Fannie.

name: Brooks, Lewis Frederick
motto: Brooksy, the big blond giant, came to us from the wilds of Derry. On campus he leads a most checkered career, being first the gentleman in the Buick, then - he makes life happy for Jack Grant. We know Lewis is a gentleman because he prefers blondes; or rather, a blonde. Let's go snowshoeing, Lewis.

name: Brooks, Randall
motto: Mike came to us from the University of Vermont at the beginning of his Sophomore year. His hobby is Zoology, and we believe Professor Jackson says that Mike will be one of the great physicians some day. He hasn't been much of a ladies' man here, and we believe he must have some fair one waiting for him in the cold north country from which he hails.

name: Brown, Margaret Frances
motto: Brownie came to us from Robinson Seminary, a shy, bashful little girl, and with the ability of sharking all of her courses. As for sharking her courses, she still continues to do so, but as for being a shy and bashful little girl now - we wonder!!

name: Buckley, John Ogden
motto: Coming to our campus in 1924, unheralded and unknown, Buck has since proven that you can't keep a good man down. To say that he is well known on the campus would be but repeating a fact. His sunny smile and disposition have made friends for him everywhere, they have even passed him in a couple of terms of Phys. Ed. So here's to Buck, may his success be confined only to the extent of our well wishes.

So here's to Buck, may his success be confined only to the extent of our well wishes.

name: Burdett, Miriam Lois
motto: If anybody wants to know anything you will hear: 'Let's ask Lois, she'll know.'
description: Lois "sharks" all her courses; well, you will understand if you know she comes from Cushing. She used to have an interest in farming, but lately her tastes have changed. Lois certainly succeeds in anything she undertakes.

name: Burke, William Michael
motto: The 'Stretch' has changed to 'Sugar' since a certain night in Dover last spring.
description: "Sugar" ran for the office of "Mayor of Durham" this fall — or rather ran from it. No one was sure which. At any rate, he is quite a runner, as Coach Sweet will testify.

name: Burpee, Eldora Haines
motto: Every time we mention 'Yab,' 'Dodie' shyly smiles and blushes.
description: "Dodie" is the prim little Commuter from Exeter with a heart of gold. The heart no longer belongs to her but to a certain party down at New Haven.

name: Carpenter, Marion Heath
motto: Pearlie's unfailing good nature enables her to take a 'ride' with unruffled temper.
description: "Pearlie" — how she hates that name! But, once you know her, you cannot help but appreciate it, for it is symbolic of many of her qualities. Although her poise and dignity of bearing sometimes fail her on such occasions, they are usually a source of great envy to her more flighty associates.

motto: If it hadn't been for that girl in Dover the 'U' might have profited more or less by Tommie's presence here.
description: "Tom" is somewhat of a card shark, and he keeps the commuting Freshmen in line. Can he annex rides to Dover? Just watch and find out.

name: Gladys, May Castle
motto: Glad is the best girl pitcher in U. N. H. and a corking good sport in everything.
description: She never lost a game yet. There's some speed behind her balls! Ask the opposing team. Next to athletics she shines as quiet proctor of third floor of Congreve. Her immediate ambition is to get an N. H. As to her chances, her record speaks for itself.

name: Roland, Francis Chandler
motto: Baron first arrived on the campus in an old broken down Ford from Waltham.
clubs: Member of Board of Control Intra-Mural A. A.
description: Liking the looks of the place he decided to call again. His greatest weakness is nurses. He goes home once in a while to call on "friends." We all know who his "friends" are. He fell once for a co-ed but now he spends his time in Bean Town.

name: Harlan, Samuel Cleveland
motto: Cleve is the little 'Commons Sheik.'
description: He's got that smoky complexion, those brown eyes, and that sandy hair that the girls all love. He almost does, but doesn't quite. Sharking History Courses is his meat, and between times he slings a wicked tray.

name: Colburn, Eunice
motto: Guess We Gotta Go Now.
description: Not "over the road to the poor house" for Eunice, even though she begs and begs and begs! Is there another in the class that has something to do all the time and is always busy? Nevertheless, a prosperous future is predicted for the ambitious one.

name: Coleman, George Ephriam Jr.
description: If Wealth is counted by friendship, "Jim" surely is the man. His winning smile and pleasing personality are two of his many assets. His love is divided between his Alma Mater and our sister institution at Orono. We are sure that his power of determination will make him successful. He is not one of the Gentlemen that prefer blondes.

name: Collins, Ethel Mary
description: We cannot all be great, not all of us are destined to be small. There is a place for each of us in this wide, wide world of ours. But when we come right down to brass tacks, Ethel fills her own little sphere of influence to perfection. Quiet, unassuming, studious, unobtrusive - all the qualities which the cold world disregards at first glance. But if you were to ask me, I should say that Ethel has more chance for success in this world than the pleasure-seekers who think only of "eat, drink, and be merry, for tomorrow we may die."

name: Conant, Malcolm Willey
description: "I have come with a word of warning!" These startling words ushered our little "Pat" over the threshold of this great University. He is always ready to do anything but reduce the representation of Canterbury in the State Legislature. His ability as a runner in his one and only boxing bout caused his fraternity brothers to send him out for track in the spring. Nevertheless, "Pat" is a real friend, and we shall look for him among the leaders.

motto: The best is yet to come.
description: She wields the hockey stick, and you should see her fingers tickle the piano keys—they rattle jazz or classics with equal facility. She is the dependable sort and we expect big things of her.

name: Cotton, Chester Arthur
motto: The best is yet to come.
description: He is one of these boys who believe in the wine, women, and song theory, as taught in the Metropolis of Newmarket. He always connects, even in the squared circle, where his nerve is exceeded only by his endurance. He's a bear for punishment.

name: Cotton, Dana Meserve
motto: In the old Frat. House he will ever come and go With his pipe and his books and his 'Hello Jo!'
description: A familiar figure on the campus; a ready smile, a great hand-clasp and the ready vernacular of the racetrack, and remarkable tales of the Lumber Camp. Oftimes disappearing to re-appear at Wellesley. We foresee his future based on the past.

name: Cuddire, Lawrence Joseph
motto: The best is yet to come.
description: He is another one of the Bay State boys who has seen fit to grace the campus with his presence. If you would like to know when Napoleon crossed the Delaware, or when Maximilian successfully invaded Durham, just ask Larry. He is a major in the History department and a private in the Military department.

name: Cummings, James Peaslee
motto: "Smiling Jim" came to New Hampshire after commuting from Manchester to Nashua for high school. After his arrival here he commuted from Fairchild to all classes, and the train was too often late. "Jim" is possessed of a perfect disposition, and all his classmates who know him like him. Seldom, if ever, does "Jim" walk on the campus without wearing a smile.

name: Currie, Alexander Blackwood
motto: "ALEC" came to college just to be near a certain somebody, but once here he didn't waste time making himself known. He's Scotch - that makes him a soccer player, but where he got that knock-out wallop is still a mystery. A winning smile and dancing eyes make him one of the most popular fellows in his class.

name: Currier, Alton Chauncey
motto: Of 1927 by choice, but a 1928 man by necessity. "Al" finally survived the ravages of time and a hard winter and caught up with the running. A resident of the old days of the Barracks and vicinity, he early made himself obvious through an uncanny aptitude to put the ball in the basket. Since then, however, heavy courses and plenty of them have been rather a "strain" on him.

name: Currier, Charles Albert
motto: "Charlie" tries to make us think that he is quiet and bashful, but everyone knows differently. Why does he spend so much time in Dean Case's office? - The Dean has a new stenographer.

name: Custeau, Emile Michael
description: Custeau has had varied experiences with the women and sharks his courses for Phi Kappa Phi. Next year we'll hear from him as mentor in Harvard Law School. He can't bear to be away from home altogether, so he commutes from Rochester.

name: Daggett, Albert Frederick
description: After two years of undivided attention to his studies "Fred" divided his attention unequally and unfairly to his studies. His mind wandered down the coast to Portland, but still his habits are strong upon him, so one can usually find him among his test tubes at Conant. The honor societies didn't know him when he came here, but the few who don't know him now will soon recognize his ability. He has set his mark high, let's wish him luck in reaching it.

name: Daland, Richard Williams
description: "Dick" came to us from Holderness where we are inclined to believe he learned that "to have is to hold." He is one man in our class who is fleet of foot, and enjoys leading the pack in the quarter-mile. His ability as a football man was checked by an accident, for which we are all sorry.

name: Danforth, Raymond
description: The little "Yes, yes, man," a bundle of smiles, and the Matron of the Barracks, all in one, are rolled up in "Ray." When he isn't afoot, he's on horseback, and there's always the flash of a curly red head following him wherever he goes. Everything so to speak is "Squee" with "Ray." Ask "Joe" Barker!

name: Daniels, Jessie Isabel
description: "Jess" is the quiet, reserved type of young lady. "Math shark" and "Physics star"—enough said, for her conscientiousness. "Jess" is susceptible to no vice, not even moved by the wink of a masculine eye. We know that her happy disposition and pleasing humor will contribute much to her success. "Jess", we wish you luck!

name: Davis, Dorothy Helen
description: We can't learn much about "Dot." She always says: "I really haven't time, I'm very busy." You'll have to interview her yourself.

name: Davis, Evalyn Mabel
description: Now one sees her, now one doesn't! That is Evalyn. She may be small, but, oh my! Flitting here and there, always busy, always happy, it would be hard to find a girl who "gets more" out of college life. Not merely content with scholastic honors, she has made both hockey and soccer teams. We congratulate you, Evalyn, and wish you similar success when you go forth to meet the cold, cruel world. It ought to prove a willing victim to your sunny smile.

name: Delbianco, Angelo Natale
description: "Del" does not imply that he came from "The Diamond State" but he surely has some of the characteristics of the diamond. Can it be that he is bright? Hardly that, for although it has not been proved, it is rumored that once he received a grade as low as eighty-five. He seems to be a bit out of place down with the Pugilists, but we are given to understand that he was born in a section of the Capitol city where men are men.

name: Dexter, Edward Augustus
description: "Dex" did not like the city life so he has moved to his new home on Athletic Park where the hens are more numerous. He comes from the region where "men are men and women never grow." Best of luck old man in your chicken business.

name: Dillon, Gomer Stanley
clubs: Platoon Sergeant R. O. T. C. (3)
description: Now Gomer Dillon is far different from most of his classmates. Not but what he enjoys himself as much as any of them, but, you see, he seeks quiet enjoyment. "Dill" is the author of several anti-petting resolutions which, if carried out in the proper manner, will revolutionize the social ethics of the University. Best of luck, "Dill," in this "quiet drive." We hope you find a new definition of the word Pet.

name: Dionne, Isabelle
description: A lot of her time is taken up by talking and she has a mean vocabulary. However, between outbursts, she finds time to play soccer, basketball, swim, etc. "Izzie" has her artistic side too. "Station S.M.I.T.H. broadcasting. Stand by and you will hear Miss Dionne render a selection on the violin." Let's all give an individual for "Izzie." Hip! Hip!

name: Dixon, Paul J.
motto: "Thought, to be effective, must be translated into action."
clubs: Chairman Membership Committee of University Bible Class (2)
description: Here is another man who, lacking the ability and time for major sports, turned his attention to the development of the social and religious side of college students. After entering college as a poultryman, Paul became scared of the "chickens" and transferred to the horticultural department. We have it on good authority that the governing force behind Paul's diversified college life is a certain young lady in Dover.

name: Dodge, Charles Eben
motto: Fireman, Save My Child.
description: He lives at the disreputable fire-station, but he is a good kid for all that. He not only sharks "Doc" Richard's courses, works his way through college, and cooks his own meals, but he finds time for co-eds and athletics on the side. We'll never forget the time he lost his shoe in a mile race and finished second after running half a mile barefooted.

name: Donovan, Margaret Elizabeth
description: We wonder why "Donnie" likes to stay over nights this year. At first, it was because she had to play hockey at 7 A.M., but after hockey season, "Donnie" still stayed in Durham about three nights a week!! "Donnie" is very athletic and likes her "Jim" both days and evenings.

name: Doucette, Marion Gertrude
description: "Anyone want to play bridge?" That was Marion's favorite pastime around the dorm—when she wasn't expounding her views on some momentous subject to an appreciative audience. For she certainly was convincing, especially after taking Mr. Ufford's debating course. We missed Marion week-ends, but she found Hampton Beach more attractive than Durham—and no wonder!

name: Drew, Frederick Wentworth
description: Although universally known by the name "Beaker," no one seems to know where and under what conditions he received it. However, he seems to have great interest in those members of the opposite sex who are endowed with red hair. He is usually seen trying to wreck some machinery or apparatus about which he doesn't know anything. Thus far he has found out what he wanted to know without any serious results, and we hope that he will be as lucky in the future.

name: Duffy, Peter Andrew
motto: "How's to get the milk, Duff?" Don't always be "weezin' out of it."
description: Your foot is O. K. now. We've always been grateful to "Andy" for being "Tommy's" benchmen in riding those unruly Freshmen. Did you ever wear them yourself, Duffy? By the way, don't you know the "tale of two cities" by heart, by this time?

name: Eadie, James
description: When the steaming monster pulled into Durham in the fall of '24, the sheik of seven counties swung from the rear coach and he's been swinging back and forth ever since between a pile of books and Dover. "Jim" started his career in Ballard Hall, but being forced out by the fair sex, he now lives in the famous Barracks. "Jim" is a terror with the women, having spoken to at least twelve in the last three years.

name: Eastman, Clifford Herman
description: Toot! Toot! Out of the way, and a slim figure appears around the bend pounding on all four. "Clif" made his debut in the running game his Freshman year, and has never stopped or been stopped since. We think he gets his ability from the fact that where he comes from, they have to run three or four miles to school every morning. At any rate, he has many times proved himself to be one of Coach Sweet's steadies.

name: Eastman, Edson Farnum
description: "Red" - not "Red" Grange - escaped from the "Pere" at West Concord and then took refuge in Durham, where he succeeded in bullying Albert into giving him a janitorship in Ballard before Ballard was lost to the girls. "Red" was transferred to the Barracks where he now holds forth as proctor. "Red" certainly would be a success in the business world. Ask him about the big bargain he found in Detroit. However, he is now planning to bring the Dairy industry back on its feet, and we have great faith in the ability of the little boy with the big smile.

name: Eastman, Hazel Mae
motto: Kimball Union Academy can well be proud of 'Hazie's' dramatic abilities. It is doubtful however, if she continues in that profession very long because — well, yes, it's a man. A witty answer to the cleverest 'ride' is always at 'Hazie's' command and futile is the attempt to compete with her strong defence. For impulsive 'Hazie' is everyone's friend and always ready to offer her services; especially when 'gardens' are concerned.

name: Elliott, Charles Ned
motto: We wonder how he does it — baseball, basketball, cross-country, editor of the GRANITE, and a 90 average. If anyone has anything they want done, they take it to Ned, and it is done. A few years from now there will be a new name in WHO'S WHO under the letter E, and it does not take a wizard to guess whose name it will be. Ned is O. K. in every way. All who once know him never cease to know him.

name: Emery, Winston Frank
motto: 'Win' is the lion who tamed the 'Lamb.' He sure knows his bananas. The Alpha Chi O's have adopted him or else he just hangs around Ben's for the fun of it. Personally we think it's the girl in the blue 'tam.' Too bad you're so quiet 'Win.'

name: Engel, Arnold
motto: When the west wind blew in the fall, 'Louis' blew in too and has been blowing ever since, not to mention falling. But a fall in time makes the world go around, even though its continual spinning makes some people dizzy. In other words 'Louis' plays a trumpet and in a cosmopolitan way — radio, vaudeville, dance, or what you will. He never made a debut in music circles — just grew up with it.

name: English, James Hugh
motto: It's a downright fact! If you don't know English, never study a foreign language.
description: Mr. English, the Valentino of the campus, is also the Willie Hoppe of the pool parlor. He hopes to fashion his footsteps to follow those of Alois Merke, and his amazing discovery. He can't quite hang himself on his line, but will probably extend it if hard pressed. The boys will all tell you that "Duke" is one mighty good gent.

name: Esersky, Etta Rose
motto: A good sport and a good friend is Etta.
description: She is one of those busy bodies who still can always find time to help someone out of a difficulty. She can do anything from cooking to skating (especially skating) and how would the basement gang ever get up in the morning without Etta?

name: Evans, Lloyd Llewellyn
motto: Sammy has an eye for music, and also for home plate which combination is apt to be sufficient.
description: The only disadvantageous point about Llewellyn is his name, but even so, names are superficial, which statement is large but just as true. But "Sammy" is a pitching ace, and this can account for a great deal.

name: Evans, Walter Horace
motto: Among the foremost athletes and soccer players"—and then "Walt" came into view.
description: Between Soccer, Jack Grant's, and deer hunting, it is estimated that "Walt" devotes two hours a week in the fall term to study. Few people realize that "Walt" has one of the strongest voices on the campus. Just recently he broke two light bulbs on the top floor of Fairchild when he shouted in the hall on the first floor. "Walt's" only failing is his love for passionate socks. During the winter a vivid shade of green made him conspicuous in Winter Sports.

name: Ferguson, John Edwin
description: Now there's "Fatty" Ferguson; not bad at all for a husky boy who would appear, if you judged from his shining pate, to be getting rather well along in years. John isn't very loquacious regarding the attractions of the home town, but "there's something fishy in Denmark." Why? Well, John used to love that little ditty; "She's a cornfed" some kind of a girl, but she's mamma to me.

name: Fields, Dorothy Amelia
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Nominating Committee (2)
Y. W. C. A. Social Committee (3)
description: Rosy cheeks, hair that's the envy of every co-ed on the campus, and a smile that will stand all kinds of weather - that's Bud! She leads a busy life, but sometimes she can take her mind off from her studies long enough to put it on - a Ford! One of her pet diversions is to walk to Dover with her fraternity sisters, but they find that it is hard work to get her beyond the corner by the Delta Pi House.

name: Fifield, Mildred
motto: Good things come in small packages
description: Because summer vacation is so short, Bowdoin gets more of her time than New Hampshire in the winter. Our little friend can keep up a conversation with anyone from a truck driver to a member of the faculty. Her athletic ability is surpassed only by her literary contributions to the Independent, her article having attracted state-wide attention.

name: Fifield, Stanley Clarence
motto: A man with a smile is a man worth while
description: Here he is, ladies - from the coal mines of Chicago, St. Louis, Kalamazoo, and other foreign seaports. He embodies everything desirable in manhood. By his smile you shall know him - he has made those words, "Grin and bear it," famous. Therefore, we dubbed him "Sunny."

name: Flanerty, Edna Grace
motto: A winning way, a pleasant smile
description: Just at the opportune moment she will offer a suggestion that can always be depended upon—then at another time she entertains us by allowing her subtle humor to escape, although a blush and a ready smile will follow.

name: Flint, Margaret Merrill
motto: A mind at peace with all below, A heart whose love is innocent
description: Queen Margaret and King Louis—what could be sweeter? May the feet of the Foote's never trip on the Flints of the stones of life. All honor to Margaret—where honor is due.

A mind at peace with all below, A heart whose love is innocent

name: Flanagan, Katherine Patricia
description: Quiet, demure, unassuming—these and various other adjectives might be used in referring to "Kay." Yet, with all her modesty, she has found time to shine on the Women's Varsity Debating Team, in "Le Cercle Francais" and various other honorary societies.

name: Fisk, Maynard Clark
motto: One ball on the side
description: After shoveling black diamonds for the Maine Central Railroad for a year or so, "Chink" decided that there was something lacking both in himself and also in the University of New Hampshire. He therefore decided to fill both wants at one time, and was duly deposited by the B. & M. in front of Morrill Hall. His oriental features and winning smile have made many friends for him, and we expect he will either be Head Forester or Champion Pool Player of the World.

name: Fogg, Bessie Lakin
motto: The best is yet to come.
description: Bessie came to us as an innocent, simple-minded little girl from Hancock, trusting all men, as all men in Hancock are to be trusted, but in Durham it is different. Bessie has changed, and has threatened to become a man-hater. We wonder?

name: Foss, Alice Mildred
motto: The best is yet to come.
description: "Al" is the type of girl all should know, a game sport and yet a student. Between her Home Ec. labs, the basketball games, and the attractions of Dover, there is no time for other amusements. But why she elected the "Home Wrecker's" course is still a question.

name: Francoeur, Jeffrey Francis
motto: The best is yet to come.
description: Fresh from a Prep. school where girls do not exist and where the use of a gymnasium is strictly for athletics, he drifted among the females. "Jeff" gave up the idea of acquiring a thorough education early in the game and devoted his talent to dancing, imitating "Ned Wayburn." He danced his way through school and no doubt will continue to do so through life.

name: French, Alvin Watson
motto: The best is yet to come.
description: "Plumpy" comes from Salisbury, and we don't mean Amesbury either. He is cheerfulness personified, always smiling. His figure earns his nickname - and they say that he is quite a sheik when in his own salt air environment. He'll make a name for himself in the world of art and architecture some day.

name: French, Chauncey Wentworth
motto: "Frenchy" is a Pre-med and a darned good one. All his roommate lacks is a nurse. Five cents a call is cheap enough, and it is a Scotchman that knows it. He is a good "Doc" except in replacing shoulders, and then he has to call for help. All he lacks for doctors' implements is a saw or a sledge hammer. He used to be a runner, but the only thing he runs after now is "Tim" and there he sure can travel.

name: Garner, Robert Sherlock
motto: Ps-s-s-s HOT LIPS "Step Right Up and Call Me Speedy" is my motto, says "Bobby."
description: He hails from the big town of Methuen, and says that they have some good women down there. We sometimes have to question his taste. By trade he is a musician, and he well emphasizes this fact by appearing now and then with a new orchestra. They say that the one he has developed this time is pretty good, and, of course, the credit all goes to "Bobby."

name: Garvey, Leo Francis
motto: "A man is no higher than his Ideals"
description: Aside from a tendency toward pugilism and a distinct liking for Chorus girls and country clubs our friend seems none the worse for wear throughout these long years. Although he has remained constant to his first love in Durham, we hear he is a man with a past. Many tales have come to our ears from "Wayside Inns" that start in Danvers and end somewhere in Rhode Island.

name: George, Avery Brewster
motto: "Still waters run deep"
description: Here we have a quiet and studious chap who seldom voices his opinion, and sees the wee small hours of the morning over the print of a chemistry book. Day in and day out, from morn till night, you may see him at his studies.

name: Goodwin, Edwin Alonzo
motto: Ed hopes to reorganize the trolley line between Somersworth and Dover after receiving his B. S. He tells us little about his affairs with the fair sex, but we know he must be popular. New Hampshire's Edison has a great future ahead of him.

name: Goodwin, Marion Louise
motto: Silence is wise. Shyness a virtue—sometimes. Content is what counts—know Marion. Baldheaded men might profit by an interview with her. 'Eat—you only live once'—theorizes Marion. 'Mislaid something?' Marion will find it. 'Forgotten anything?' Don't worry. Marion will remember for you.

name: Goold, Pierce Edmund
motto: Goold came to the campus last year from the University of Maine. Since then he has upheld all forms of winter sports—and indoor sports. Proficiency in ski-jumping and smoking a pipe are his outstanding achievements—so far as we know.

name: Gove, Ruth Alice
motto: Modest, shy, retiring little Miss—maybe! To those who really know, she is anything but. Ask her about that 'frat' pin she wears and she'll say, 'Oh, that! Santa Claus gave me that!' It must be nice to have lived to such an age and still believe in Santa Claus. But then, if we all got daily letters from Indiana, we might believe in Santa, too.

name: Greenough, William
description: When "Bill" first struck our wonderful campus he was immune from the fair sex. But since then he has succumbed to the wiles of a co-ed. He now "Bud's" out regularly, and it looks like a sure thing. "Bill" is an engineer, and we all feel that he will engineer the Varsity Track Team to perfection. His ability as a student and as a lover have been very successful so far, and with this same spirit he should certainly be successful in future undertakings.

name: Griffin, Kelsea
description: Did you ever wonder where that phrase: "Never say can't!" originated? Her powers are seemingly inexhaustible; she sketches a little better, thinks a little deeper, and goes just a little further than anyone else in everything. From such a description one would expect to meet a rather over-powering, boastful sort of individual; but who could be more reserved outwardly, more likeable, and more of an all 'round good sport than "K"?

name: Gulliver, Reginald
description: All ostriches don't have feathers. Ostrich Gulliver hasn't and there are no flies on him either. "Gully" hails from Needham and believe me, he is some boy. He isn't Scotch, but he sure can hang on to the shekels, and as for borrowing rubbers he has them all stopped. There is a girl down in Smith we hear. There is something mysterious about her, but she is there just the same, because "Gully" says so and he always tells the truth. Nevertheless he makes a darned good roommate. The only trouble is he wears a 14½ shirt.

name: Guptill, George Herbert Jr.
motto: All is not Gold that Glitters
description: Neither does size count with this man. He sure knows his "Stuff" and we all envy him. His many friends and remarkable abilities warrant a brilliant future. He often tells us of his high school days at dear old Raymond High.

name: Gustafson, Clarence Henry
description: Clarence probably achieved the greatest fame of his career in college on the personnel force of the Military Art Department, when he assigned all of the Theta Chi boys to guard duty. That was a masterpiece, "Gus." His sense of humor is well exercised and that Swedish laugh sure makes the thing a pleasure.

name: Haines, Walter Blake
description: Walt is one of these "little fellows" with a big wide smile. He is built like a football player, but lacks weight. As a quarterback he would be a failure. 33-66-888-try it! He had ambitions to be an architect, but - it's lucky he never got started on the women.

name: Haley, Harold Edward
description: Haley is our "little old man" from the cultured town who tells us everything from the Revolutionary war of Babcock's interpretation to what is trumps according to his own ideas. We could not honestly say that he is wild about the women, but the quiet men are those to be watched.

name: Hallisey, Mildred Louise
motto: "The best laid plans of mice and men -"
description: Let Milly finish it for she can quote anything- even from life. But when it comes to "Gym!" Well, Milly was entered in the tennis tournament until Doc. Griffin rescued her from this horrible ordeal. For sometimes there's consolation even in "heart trouble."

name: Hammond, Ruth Wilson
motto: Ruth hates nicknames and has therefore emitted the most potent of her many 'Noms de plume.'
description: She is so fast she has to start the day before to catch up with herself. You'll get there just the same, Ruthie dear, and we all know that you'll be the best little Home Ec'er ever.

name: Hanagan, John Joseph
motto: 'Joe has got them eyes, this nose, etc.' and he only needs a shovel to complete the picture.
description: We hear he has invented a machine to roll flat all hills. He intends to try this in Somersworth, so that it will be easier for him to get up to her house. "When I graduate,-" says "Joe."

name: Hanson, Elizabeth Frances
description: A reserved and unassuming young Miss is Betty, going around with her books under her arm. Yes, she's a "Home Ecker" not "Wrecker" because—well, it's surprising how these quiet people get ahead of the rest of us. We certainly wish you the best of luck, Betty!

name: Harriman, Carl Edward
motto: 'Quiet, men, quiet.' These are the memories Carl leaves with us.
description: He is the man who breaks the roads to Smith Hall in the winter, and breaks all the rules in the summer. Yes, Carl is quiet, but these still waters, you know, run deep. Sorry for the crude simile, Carl, old boy.

name: Hatch, Ervin Nerva
description: A potent man and a good man even if he did come from Jackson, New Hampshire. "Hatchie" has never fallen for the wiles of the Co-eds, and believe us that is going some. Lots of success in future years, old boy, and we are all glad to have spent our days at New Hampshire with you.

name: Hatch, Harris
description: Although not officially registered as a commuter, "Pete" leaves the campus many evenings. Sometimes to get something at home, sometimes to have his tooth fixed, and sometimes he admits it is to see Mary. However, he does find time to go out for Varsity Football and Hockey in between times, and we admire a man that can go to Newmarket and take part in sports at the same time. Anyway, "Pete" always finishes anything he starts, and in this game of life I'll bet it on him.

name: Hatch, Herbert Oren Jr.
clubs: Second Lieutenant R. O. T. C. (3)
description: "The Brewster Flash," otherwise known as "Snubber," hails from way-back somewhere. Since he came to Durham, his time has been divided between making trips to Keene and writing letters to someone in Montpelier. We have a feeling that her real name is Marjorie. Congeniality is the "Midget's" keynote.

name: Hawkins, Hubert Wheeler
description: "Bud" says that the best thing about his musical ability is the keen time he has occasionally at the dances. Hawkins stars in the Glee Club and all music courses. His faithful attendance to these classes makes him "Bob" Manton's "left-hand man." His favorite jazz piece is: "You Never Can Tell What a Red-Haired Mama Will Do."

name: Hayes, Milton Ware
motto: Cappy is known as the 'student.' He spends more time in Conant Hall than any other fellow in college, and intends some day to be a great 'chemist.' However, not all his energy is spent indoors, for last winter he strutted his stuff on the rink and exhibited rare form in the game of hockey.

name: Hebert, Alfred Anthony
motto: The 'King' abdicated his throne at St. Anselm's after a year's reign, and came down to join the boys at New Hampshire. If he ever regretted the move, no one would suspect it who ever heard his hearty laugh echo through Hetzel Hall at about 2 A.M. If there is one thing for which the 'King' is justly famous, it is birthday parties. Whether the party is his or not, everybody is sure to have a whacking good time, and somebody is sure of wearing cushions the next day.
text: The 'King' abdicated his throne at St. Anselm's after a year's reign, and came down to join the boys at New Hampshire. If he ever regretted the move, no one would suspect it who ever heard his hearty laugh echo through Hetzel Hall at about 2 A.M. If there is one thing for which the 'King' is justly famous, it is birthday parties. Whether the party is his or not, everybody is sure to have a whacking good time, and somebody is sure of wearing cushions the next day.

name: Hemingway, Ellis Lewis
motto: Chink is one of those ultra-modern boys, one of those who would like to, but don't-quite-dare-to kind. So far he has managed to elude the ensnaring ways of the co-eds, but we understand that although he walks in Durham, his heart beats in Chelsea. It is a treat to listen to him expound his theories against 'free love' for 'Chink' is the one of the few who uphold the Puritan ideal of marital obligations. He is also a true Cosmopolitan, owing to his birth on the island of Singapore. Of course, he wasn't responsible for that, so we can't hold that against him.

name: Higgins, George Warren
motto: Back in the dim and distant past when men were men, the ancestors of George Higgins originated that well-known statement, 'There ain't no Santa Claus.' This was carried down from generation to generation until 'Higg' came to school. Even then, George believed it for two years. But now, he is the sole owner of the candy counter at the Theta Chi house.

name: Hildreth, Malcolm Dee
motto: Mac comes to us from Plymouth, the town that manufactures athletic goods. Mac wears athletic underwear himself. He started to prove his ability as a sprinter, but his feet were too fast for his body, and he grew an inch at every practice. He didn't want to get too tall, so he left organized athletics and took up soccer. One would have to go a long way to find a better fellow than Mac.

name: Hill, Henry Bertram
motto: Humpy has demonstrated that 'Keep to the Wright' is a good principle to follow throughout life. At any rate, one always knows where to find him. He looks innocent - and may be at that. Anyhow, he is a sweet lad.

name: Hobbs, Llewellyn Frank Jr.
motto: Frank hails from the wilds of North Hampton where he isn't mayor for the simple reason that they do not have one. He is pursuing the science of Chemistry as his life work, and all indications point to his being successful. Some one once heard him say that he believed in co-education. We wonder why!! Despite his playful nature, Von is a very fine fellow.

name: Hoffs, Barbara Sterling
motto: Prof: - 'What is laughter?' Student: - 'Ask Barb, she knows.' Never ill. Always singing. Always laughing. Does she blush? - Inquire!!

name: Hoitt, Dorothy Emma
motto: Be Good and You'll be Happy
description: "Dot" not only lives up to her motto but she surely exercises her oratorical ability in helping her friends along that straight and narrow path. Psychologically speaking, we wonder if her motherly instinct is inborn or if it has been acquired since that famous flashlight episode. "Dot" attended summer school this last summer. Was it fruitful? Judge for yourself. Well, she became a member of the Scabbard and Blade anyway.

name: Hoitt, Samuel Waldo
description: "Sammy," being a native Durhamite, has a thorough working knowledge of the surrounding country which is valuable not only to himself but to others. His professors say he is a very apt student and we are inclined to believe this is true in more ways than one. He tells us he came to college just to finish his education which many will vouch is very, very liberal. Success in something or other is assured him if we are to believe in signs.

name: Horn, Elizabeth Agnes
description: "Oh, I'll have it done," and she does, although we'd like to know how she does so much in so short a time. You should see Betty on the skating rink;—her feet can fly as fast as her hands. P.S. She's a "Home-Wrecker" which means?

name: Horne, Frank Wescott
description: Frank's career has been marked largely by spasmodic absences of his fraternity pin. It is rumored about the campus that Cecil B. DeMille saw "Tillie of Bloomsbury" from the front row. He was unable to sign up Frank because our hero wishes to become a sailor. His line is long enough so that he could hang himself from the highest mast in the world.

name: Horne, Ruth Frances
motto: Forward and frolic glee was there, the will to do, the soul to dare
description: Smiling, with a cheery word ever on her lips, ready to comfort anyone who's feeling blue; sympathetic, and with a very soft spot for the "under dog"; clever, but not boastful. Whether studying, writing rhymes or livening up a dull party with her wit, every moment of her everyday life, she is always willing to take time to help others.

name: Hoyt, Raymond Albert
motto: Much wisdom often goes with the fewest words
description: "Ray" has many "gifts that take our eyes"; his ability to play baseball, his good looks, and his wonderful voice we think of first. Willing to help, always kind, liked by all, and with high ideals — that is "Ray."

name: Hunt, Paul Merryman
description: "Mike" is one of the shining lights in our class, not only because he has red hair, but because he is always doing something — besides his lessons. He is a "Greek" of three dimensions and "toots" a mean trombone in the Band. Besides this, he is always managing something.

name: Huntoon, Grovenor Ariel
description: Far away in New Hampshire at the junction of the two railroads that are the pride of the B. and M. lives Grovenor Huntoon. As a boy, "Huntie" used to while away the time on the banks of the mighty Contoocook River. His love for nature and the river has never left him. No doubt, he thought last spring that there was a whale up there above the mill dam. One finds it mighty hard to catch whales in a canoe.

name: Hutchinson, Harold Curtis
motto: "Chipper" stole in from Wilton, and remained so unobtrusive his Freshman year that the proctor called him the quietest fellow in the Barracks. Later his room became quite a hang-out for bridge games. "Chipper" is a regular Bill Johnston on the tennis court. Just play him some time. He always manages to do his studying too, in spite of his "uke," chess games, and the inevitable movies; and there will be a nice low numeral in front of his name on that graduation program.

name: Jack, Gerald Albion
motto: "Jerry" is really rather a smooth lad, but he also has other distinctions. He is the best dancer in his fraternity, which is saying something; he is the best soda-jerk in his class, which is saying more; and he is the most prolific letter-writer in the University, which is saying a great deal. All in all, "Jerry" will pass muster with flying colors.

name: Jackson, Lewis Leonard
motto: They have murders in Lebanon. We don't know whether "Jack" ever figured in any of them, but we are watching him for traces of barbarism. As Head Proctor of Fairchild, "Jack" makes a wonderful coach of parlor rugby. "Jack" also takes that well known course of LIBRARY, from which he claims to get stacks and stacks of practical experience. "Jack" gains his popularity by his winning smile across Jack Grant's hash counter.

name: Jaquith, Lester Gibson
motto: "Jake" began his college career at Worcester Tech - but then, sensing the error of his ways, he came to New Hampshire. Clinton, Massachusetts, the home of the one-way street, is also "Eppie's" camping ground. "Jake" says that when he completes his engineering course at New Hampshire, he is going to apply principles of friction to banana peelings.

name: James, Clifford Edward
description: This flaming youth came to college to get polished, and has frequently achieved this goal. "Beany" will probably never set the world on fire, but the fire in his soul finds fuel galore at Smith College where he has made numerous pilgrimages. We all know what a sensation "Cliff" creates at Congreve whenever passing by, but woe be unto him if tight trousers become the vogue again and show those affectionate knees that simply can't seem to keep away from each other. Good luck, "Cliff!" The butter and egg business is looking for men like you.

name: Johnson, Elizabeth
description: "Johnnie" looks quiet and unassuming, but those who are acquainted with her know otherwise. "Johnnie" came to us the usual man hater, but now we notice her very great interest in and attachment to a distant "Henry"; also her favorite dessert seems to be, in recent times, Delta Pi(e). Nevertheless, we like her and hope for her success and happiness in all her "walks" of life.

name: Johnson, Stanley Pulcifer
description: After learning all there is to know about Horticulture, "Pul" expects to return to Hampstead and make his fortune crossing strawberries with watermelons. The night life of Durham holds no lure for this lad. 'Tis said that he had been here two years before he discovered that he was attending a co-educational University. He may be unknown at Smith and Congreve, but he is a general favorite at Barracks B where he hangs out.

name: Joslin, George Elias
description: "Goose," a keen lad from Keene, N. H., gave the girls the "once over" upon his arrival and then decided to divide his time equally between the co-eds and his studies. His favorite song is "Margie," and they say he has a girl by that name, but we wouldn't want to go on record as having said that. He knows Congreve like a book because he spends his afternoons, evenings and weekends there with an occasional jaunt to the log cabin. He gets the "low down" on the chickens by taking poultry courses, and spends the rest of his spare time managing the hockey team.

name: Joy, Ruth Elizabeth
description: Ruth used to like to ride on trains, but now she has given up this pastime. Of course, "Rufus" has her likes and dislikes. Next to baseball players, she has a special liking for R. O. T. C. officers. Things are going smooth now, for the baseball player is on the campus, while the officer is far away.

name: Kelly, Pauline Joan
description: Since "Polly" came to the University, she has become greatly interested in nature and has spent much of her time in the nearby woods with the birds and squirrels and things. She has also spent a great part of her time on the broad Highway. Who could resist those freckles and that cherubic smile. So "Polly" has been around quite a lot. At bridge, she is unparalleled.

name: Kenerson, Elsie Dean
motto: Efficiency is thy name, Elsie
clubs: Junior Member of Smith Hall House Committee (3)
description: "Else" is not only efficient in her studies but in all she undertakes. She is head quiet-proctor of Smith Hall, but the girls never complain because they say; "Elsie never stays in - she is always at the movies." Her favorite course, besides co-education, is Economics. You'll have to hand it to this young lady for she is clever and witty.

name: Kenison, Frederick Damon
description: It was the fall of 1924. The train roared up to the station. "Windy" roared from the platform, "Porter take my bag." There was no porter to take his bag and he was forced to carry it to the Barracks himself. Everyone knew when he arrived. "Windy" certainly has the line common of all the north country boys. He thought North Conway was a metropolis until he saw the height of T-Hall.

name: Killeen, Elizabeth Catherine
description: That Titian Blonde! We knew her as our Freshman year, but she's to everybody now. She was so fond of train rules that she and another co-ed even tried to ride to Boston on a certain Sunday night - until by the kindness of the conductor, they found themselves stranded at the athletic field.

name: Krinsky, Marcia
motto: "What, what did you say?"
description: Oh, yes, Marcia's a good kid alright, even though she does ask a lot of questions at times. We'll have to hand it to her for getting the marks, all the same. And as for cards - well, she's a commuter!

name: Ladd, Karl Plumer
description: They say he has a girl at Wellesley, though. Karl, Harriman and their Ford, "Leaking Lena," are famous for miles around, and may be seen at all the dances. is the star broad jumper on the track team, and a fast forward on the basketball court. Rumor has it that Karl is going in the soap business when he graduates, since he has already discovered a soap mine in Dover which he thinks is a good clean proposition.

name: Lafond, Edward Francis
motto: "Dover at least once a week"
description: This lad hails from Huntington, of which Springfield, Mass. is a suburb according to him. If you see a fellow dressed in a neat looking blue serge suit with a "jazzy" sweater beneath, going to E. E. or M. E. lab. that's. Before more is said we had better stop. However is a fine sport and an all-around good fellow.

name: Lamb, Liliane Theresa
motto: "Lambie's" password is "Pardon" - not spoken in a humble tone, either. Oh, no! She could almost get along without any other word. Ask her about the ball and chain she wears on her neck - Pardon?! And who gets most of the toothpicks in Durham? Pardon?!! Why is she so interested in Dartmouth? Pardon!!!

name: Landon, Lester L. Jr.
motto: Like 'em, Love 'em, Leave 'em
description: Little "Les" Landon left Hillsboro to lead a laborious life in the lanes of learning. Aside from long letters and long toll calls from a lonely lady, he finds time to light the lamp of learning. He seems to have been quite inactive, socially, on the campus since the graduation of a certain blonde, who claimed much of his time when a freshman. Now there is a snappy school teacher in the story, but we must admit he's a judge of women. An electrical engineer he aspires to be, but Steinmetz's "rep" is safe from being overshadowed we think.

name: Lang, Mary Ellen
description: "Mayna" studies hard, dances well, is an obliging friend - and what better could you wish for in a college girl? She likes early morning trains, tennis, and kitty-whist. She says that her chief ambition is to get a "kick" out of eating at the Commons.

name: Langer, Walter Clarence
description: The Hon. Sir Lucifer, originator of "hold me up," "blamed if I am," "find me the pro," and kindred expressions, is reputed to be quite the musician, in more ways than one. But then he's not alone in this. He came originally from Manchester, but he can live that down, as many another has. We might say - sadly, seriously and to low music, that Lucy likes to play. Hey, Jack?

name: Lawry, Henry Madison
motto: The best is yet to come.
description: Henry is another one of the Dover tie-bumpers, working in the haberdashery department of the B. & M. Railroad - taking care of the ties. During his first two years, he flunked a lot of courses because he gave too much time to the Dover Charmers. However, he has seen the error of his ways and is now in a fair way of becoming a second Steinmetz.

motto: The best is yet to come.
description: "Moe" had ambitions to clean up on Fritz Kreisler, but he got stuck on a high seventh in Largo and broke a gut. Personally we believe he is in love, for every time we see him the haze is thicker and brighter blue. Some day "Moe" is going to take a vacation.

name: Lindahl, Clifford Oliver
motto: The best is yet to come.
description: "And the Queen City sent another good boy down to the University." Lindahl is one of the Three Musketeers and the other two miss him now that he is away, telling people what he accomplished while he was in college. "Cliff" is a fine fellow and we all will be glad to have him return to his own port on the Oyster River.

name: Lindsay, Roger Manus
motto: The best is yet to come.
description: Roger crawled out of the subterranean passages of Lost River and saw a tree for the first time. His soul was stirred to its very depths. Confused with wonder, he wandered onto a railroad track where he was unceremoniously picked up by the cowcatcher and carried southward. For some reason, the train stopped at Durham and Roger, seeing the college woods, knew his fate and ever since has been a devotee of Forestry.

motto: She is silent, she is shy, But there's mischief in her eye.
description: We haven't become fully acquainted with "Dot" because of her absence from school for a year, but in the short time we have known her, we are well aware of her ability to draw and her willingness to use that talent.

name: Lizio, Ralph Americo
description: Who doesn't know "Liz"? There is a question that very few can answer in a negative manner. "Liz" hails from the town of ships and shipyards. He says he was born on a boat, and that may be the reason why he rolls his eyes. "Liz" is a boxer, which may be another excuse for living in the navy town. A cheerful "Hi" and a winning smile for everybody makes him a friend to all who know him.

He says he was born on a boat, and that may be the reason why he rolls his eyes. "Liz" is a boxer, which may be another excuse for living in the navy town. A cheerful "Hi" and a winning smile for everybody makes him a friend to all who know him.

name: Lord, George
description: George didn't like the class of '27 so he stayed out a year to be with us. A worthy addition to our class, we all agree. Always good natured, a fellow who is willing to help everyone. He doesn't always get the best results but, unlike many of us, he has the stick-to-itiveness which counts in the end. He is now out for hockey in which sport he hopes to make his letter. We wish him luck.

name: Lord, Grace Lillian
description: Behold this Phys. Ed. pioneer every Saturday morning, swimming the English Channel in the Richards' laundry. The rest of the week, behold her doing cross-country work on behalf of the P. O. male. And all this seems necessary to render her strength of mind strong enough to endure a "pestiferous" room-mate.

name: Lough, Norbert Francis
description: A man who is popular on the campus even though he is not here much of the time. He solicits his way to Dover nearly every day but always returns from our suburb the following day with studies all prepared and ready for the day's battle. He is not interested in the fair sex, but is always open for improvement.

name: Lyford, Agnes Elsina
description: Yes, we'll give away the big secret - "Aggie's" hobby is playing cards! She plays through the dark hours of night 'til the wee sma' hours of morning, and she never trumps her partner's ace. To be sure, "Aggie" is a red head, but contrary to rule, she is mighty good-natured and we wish there were more like her.

name: Maclaren, Edward Wallace
motto: "Got any smoking terbaccor? No? Wal, I'll smoke m'own, then"
description: "Mac" arrived from Alstead with his dog team one September afternoon fully intending to take Reverend Lovell's place, but how he has changed! He sure makes the old saying about ministers' sons and deacons' daughters more binding. "Mac" keeps his distance from the co-eds, but frequently makes week-end trips to Worcester where he visits his brother.

name: McCrillis, Virginia Mary
motto: "For you have touched a loyal hand, Looked into brown eyes, and made a noble friend"
description: She is a corking good sport and ever ready for a good time. Her major is Home Economics and lucky are those who are ever treated to her delicacies. She has shown great appreciation of the work of our football team and particularly, loyalty to our star fullback. Here's to "Gin."

name: Mcleod, Daniel Nickol
motto: YOU FIX it
description: The Carnival King from Littleton came down from the North all equipped with his "reds." He's a big hearted boy, never borrowing, always lending. Because his name is Daniel and he hails from the great open spaces, thus the name "Boon." "Got a Butt."

name: Macphee, Donald Francis
motto: Don is a lover of out-door sports. In fact, not being satisfied with his work on the track and cross-country squads, he enlisted in the mighty army which follows tennis. In "Mac's" case, his paramount interest prompted him to stay on guard all night so that he might get out on the court at four in the morning. Such love for sport may make a second Bill Tilden of him.

name: Macy, Robert Simpson
motto: In the wilds of the College Woods there is a little cabin. Here "Bob" spends most of his time in company with ——. "Bob" would like to be a ski jumper but the skis seem to stick in the wrong place. He is from Berlin, and of course you know how the men from the north country are. Four score and ten they fall, "Bob."

name: Manning, John Marcellus
motto: John comes from the Pine Tree State where they make them rough and rugged. If John was not rugged when he arrived in Durham, he certainly will be by the time he finishes slinging hash for "Ma" Leighton in the Commons. And when it comes to studies, there are few that can compare with this curly haired boy from up-country.

name: Robert John Matthew
motto: A wheedling voice and a sidling walk make 'Bob' the subject of many a talk. We have often wondered where he got those big, dark eyes, that pointed chin, that way of wearing his clothes so well.

name: Evelyn Alice Melendy
motto: Never give up!
description: Economy is her major, men her minor. "Here, let me help you with that!" says "Al" to a struggling Math. student or theme writer, and she proceeds to save someone's life.

name: Charles Leighton Meloon
motto: The rest of this term I'm going to hit the books.
description: Aside from the fact that he has been the Chief Engineer of New Castle, we know very little about him. However, if being unlucky at cards means anything, there must be a trail of broken hearts behind him, but a Boston girl has taken charge lately, and we have just about given "Charlie" up for lost.

name: Beulah Merrill
description: At Cushing, Beulah captured all the letters awarded in athletics and since she came to New Hampshire she has made every class team that she has attempted to make. Wherever there is anything to be done, Beulah is one of the first to arrive and she always brings along her whistle and that contagious laugh.

name: Merrill, Robert Pillsbury
description: "Bob's" line has always been judging, so he says, and when he can't find any livestock upon which to concentrate his efforts, he does his particular best on the Co-eds. He is a man, and skates rings around all opposing hockey teams. The "New Hampshire" missed him when he was forced to give up work on the Staff, and we said if he would only let the Co-eds alone, all would be well.

name: Milan, Ruth Annette
description: Ever since Freshman days, Ruth has succeeded in acquiring an injury of some sort the night before an important event. Sometimes it is only a black eye and sometimes a sprained ankle. However, she always emerges from it with that characteristic grin of hers. One part of her anatomy which has not been broken is her heart.

name: Mitchell, Frederick Barr
description: Until Christmas of 1926, we believed that "Freddie" was unblemished by feminine attractions. Consequently, we were very much surprised to see the picture he received. However, "Freddie's" hair is red, and that's enough to settle any discrepancies we may have had in judging him. He is a chap who doesn't limit himself to studies alone, even though he is taking an engineering course.

name: Monat, Urgel Alcid
motto: "They are gone those old familiar faces."
description: These are the words that are apt to be sung at "Ben's" when "Mike" and those admirers of his jigs and patois are no longer to be encountered at that favorite rendezvous of students.

name: Moore, Howard Cross
description: Howard hails from Malden, the home of cyclists. He thinks nothing of pedaling around Lake Winnepesaukee and back the same day. "Howie" craves exercise, so when he has worried through his advanced French and English classes, he goes to the tennis courts where he keeps his opponent chasing beautifully placed cross court volleys. Energetic, likable, a good friend that's "Howie," and to judge by the pictures on his dresser, a connoisseur of feminine beauty.

name: Morreels, Charles Louis
description: "Chick" cannot justly be called the class grind because he does not have to struggle for his knowledge, but he certainly hits his courses. His honorary embellishments are many. His only regret in taking the engineering course is that there are so few women in his classes. As a teacher of mathematics, he will, no doubt, prove a great success.

name: Morris, Priscilla
description: "Priscilla" is the girl with the black curly hair, who sharks all her Math. courses - and because she really knows her stuff, too! She says she intends to be a Math. teacher, but with her helpful and unselfish ways, there are a number of things which we could more easily see her doing. Her snapping brown eyes and her cheerful smile are only outward manifestations of those qualities which make her friends appreciate her, and her acquaintances wish to know her better.

name: Morrison, Ralph Burnham
motto: "Tis better to have loved and lost than never to have loved at all."
description: "Gloomy Gil Dobie" Morrison, the little shadow in the Coach's office, decided that Laconia lacked something so he made Durham his objective. He is very reticent about explaining vital mysteries of the past. If there is anything the class can do to help you "Chi" just let us know.

name: Morrison, Stanley William
description: "Stan" resembles Napoleon - rather small and so different. The dentist told him that he had room for another set of teeth but that may not mean anything. An enterprising youth and an ardent worker, never been in love, and hopes he never will be, indexes "Stan" exactly. In spare moments he dabbles in soccer, journalism and similar vices.

name: Munhall, Genieve Elizabeth
description: "Gene's" nature is sweet, jolly and - "Frank!" No, she doesn't commute from Dover, she's merely a movie fan. She surely believes that opportunity knocks but once - how? with a "hammer," Gene? Well, you've broken one record anyway - Keep it up.

name: Murnane, Patrick John
description: "Pat" was once a baseball player, until studies sunk him deeper than the proverbial bottom. Now he bats for .840 in the history and Poly Sci. league. He's got the grin that gets folks. Make the best of it "Pat" and the world's yours.

name: Necker, Edward Arthur
clubs: First Sergeant R. O. T. C. (3)
description: "Eddie" has stepped into the squared ring and swapped blows with some rugged fellows since he struck Durham in the fall of 1924. For Coach Sweet, he heaves a mighty discus, and enjoys telling the phys-edders how it is done. We almost forgot - "Eddie" is also a hash-slinger in the Marshall House. We have always wondered why they don't let him in the dining room.

name: Nelson, William Pettee
motto: Bill is one of the solid, business-like fellows who become ministers or captains of finance.
description: He comes from Salt Lake City where the Mormons are, but he has not picked out any wives yet—or it may be that because he is the Dean's grandson, he keeps such activities under cover. He's president of the Mathematics Club, and in spite of his cheerful countenance, the class of 1928 is proud of him.

high school: PITTSFIELD HIGH SCHOOL, NEW HAMPTON LITERARY INSTITUTE
motto: Jimmie has been named as one of the most efficient hash slingers on the campus. At 'Jack's' he is without an equal. His one great drawback is the great attraction he has for the fairer sex.
description: He has been hard hit, but that school-boy complexion still stays with him and they continue to flock for him. "Jimmie" has a personality that can't be beat.

motto: The author of the taxi cab romance, football player, smart student, and son of a clergyman is the famous 'Pat.'
description: He carries his ecclesiastical physiognomy with him and exhibits it, to our horror, in public, but appearances have been known to deceive. They do!

name: Percival, Warren Edward
motto: The question is 'When is a puck chaser not a puck chaser?'
description: If clever work on the hockey rink entitles a fellow to skating around the pond late at night with—friends, the hockey team will have more candidates than ever next year. The Aggie school claims "Perc," but it will be hard to settle down on the farm after the strenuous life at New Hampshire.

motto: Where did I leave that book? I thought that pocketbook was under the table. Where did I put my lab. key?
description: "Nan" may arrive a little late owing to the unfortunate faculty which her room-mates have of misplacing her things - but she surely does get there!

name: Pickwick, George Bradley
motto: And that's some plumbing.
description: We'll admit that George is the Delta Pi sheik, and the moustache gives him the local color, though tent and camel he has not. He boots a wicked soccer ball, and just adores mud. The one thing in his favor is that he loves to sleep.

name: Pierce, Norman James
description: "Kilowatt" is a wizard along the lines of his hobby, and attends the University of New Hampshire when he isn't shocking folks electrically. When you see him take a whole heap of junk and turn it into a radio set that works, you will be convinced that he knows his stuff. We have heard of interests that he has in California, and we suspect that those interests are in the fair sex.

name: Pollard, Marguerite Ruth
motto: "Where'd you get those eyes?"
clubs: Freshman Commission of Y. W. C. A. (1)
description: Our Polly is modest and bashful, but oh! those eyes. And how she can use 'em! Ask the man with the—"heart." She's always a good sport with lots of pep, and we know no one who will ever put anything over on her. Her one ambition is to teach the Spaniards how to speak real Spanish. They say it isn't wise to jump at conclusions, but Polly did the broad jump and won out.

name: Pray, Dorothy Allen
motto: "A merry heart maketh a cheerful countenance"
description: Who can resist "Dot's" marvelous eyes or her charming smile? She is most versatile for she rides and swims to perfection, helps our Women's Student Government, is a fine student, and still finds time for her correspondence with Manchester.
