name: Preble, Allan Curtis
description: "Al" came down from the Bay State to get some dope on how to run the business of the world and then is going back and put his "hot dope" into practice. He helps keep up the standards of the University with his pleasing "Hi" and his attitude toward affairs in general.

name: Cavett, Oliver Prickett
description: "Cavey" has the darndest luck buying neckties. And he would like another man's woman, too. How'd we guess? Well, he said so! Shame, shame, "Cavey!" He is always well dressed, and another year will put him at the top of the social register. Excelsior!

motto: Thousands at his bidding speed
description: In "Puss's" case it is thousands at "her" bidding speed and we can't blame them in the least. "Puss" wishes some people would take a hint once in a while. "Puss" is also a Home-"Ecker" or "Wrecker," which is it, "Puss?"

name: Edwin, Herbert Putnam
description: "Put" reminds us of a little motorcycle we used to have in the days of our youth, "Put, put put," and all business until something blew out. Just think what a lot of quiet there will be at New Hampshire when the last echo dies out.

name: Muriel, Edna Quint
motto: Oh, maid of most bewitching grace, you've got the sweetest little baby face
description: That "Quintie" doesn't believe that silence is an excellent thing in woman, is very evident. She is one of our most "glib" talkers but we wouldn't want her to be otherwise, because many a time has she made us happy with her company. Who could help but envy her for her large eyes and wonderful hair? Among her other attributes is her unending generosity.

motto: Agriculture
description: "Bill" is better known as the "Pied Piper," as he blows a mean horn in the Band. He decided last year that his course was more or less monotonous, so he took a term off to recuperate. He finds plenty to do and plenty of time to do it. "Bill" is one mighty fine chap, for a' that.

motto: Liberal Arts

His snappy work in the short field is one of the reasons for the team's success. We have learned that ball players are temperamental, and if that is so, "Buck" is sure big league material.

name: Regali, Ralph Anthony
motto: Liberal Arts
description: "Rig" must be training to be a fire chief, since he does so much night research work at the University fire station. "Hot men for hot jobs," say we. This same aggressiveness characterizes "Rig" on the gridiron as well. Professional ski-jumping will probably be his later calling, since he admits that injuries are the least of his troubles because of well distributed layers of adipose tissue.

motto: J. B. another Mount Hermit. Long years in the hermitage made him immune to reduction, but his every so often pilgrimage to obtain, as he says, a little spaghetti and the fixings sounds all right, but greater assets hath no man than this— that he throw a little beef, but then it's nice to be human.

name: Reid, Helen Louise
motto: Out upon it I have loved Three whole days together
description: Helen is not fickle, she merely has the scientific attitude you would expect of a Math. major. This is the explanation of her remarkable scholastic and social career. Helen feels that she's really acquainted with Durham. Besides strolls in every direction, she is one of those unfortunates who have had formal introduction to its mud.

name: Reid, Neil Gordon
description: Neil is securing all the information he can about Animals both four- and two-legged, so as to be able to serve his state and Nation as a judge of what is what in the blood and flesh department. Neil has many friends and we all wish him the best of luck in the future.

name: Reney, Doris Maude
high school: STEVENS HIGH SCHOOL, KEENE NORMAL SCHOOL
description: We fear that Dot tries to hide her light under a bushel. Although she never ventures an opinion voluntarily, when her advice is sought she gives a well-thought-out, reasonable reply. She is a girl whose friendly manner invites friends, and consequently she has many of them.

name: Ricciardi, Richard Salvatore
description: His dancing eyes and dancing feet are what characterize "Richie." He is as much at home on one dancing surface as another. He has danced in the square ring, on the soccer field, and on the Gym floor at three out of every five informals since the fall of '24. He is always on his toes ready to meet you and eager to greet you. Everybody—Faculty and student—knows "Richie" down at Jack's.

name: Ricker, Elizabeth
clubs: New Hampshire Staff, Reporter (1), Alumni Editor (2), (3)
description: "Guess I'll cut Poly-Sci. today. Say, got any alumni news? Don't forget we have Episcopal Services this Sunday. Well, I've got to go home," and "Libby" with her "taking ways" is in and out of our midst.

name: Roberts, Isabel
description: Another transfer from the great open spaces. But Minnesota sent her here; she's not from Missouri. We wonder if she misses the bright lights of little old New York or whether Durham offers sufficient compensation for their loss. By the way, Isabel is an authority on—"The Songs We Forget to Remember."

name: Robinson, Elsie Louise
description: Elsie is a quiet commuter from Somersworth. She is majoring in history and spends all her time in the library when she isn't playing cards or "Dodging with Ray."
text: Elsie is a quiet commuter from Somersworth. She is majoring in history and spends all her time in the library when she isn't playing cards or "Dodging with Ray."

name: Robinson, William Evans
description: To look at "Bill" we see a sweet, innocent, dark-eyed youth, but when it comes to sarcasm, leave it to "Bill." He and "Doc" Bowler run a close race. When a freshman, "Bill" was a student pulling a whale of an average, but since then he's met his Miss Waterloo and — well you know the rest.

name: Rogers, John Edward
description: Whether on the gridiron, hockey rink, or Smith Park, John tries his best — and usually succeeds. We don't know what will become of suburban social life while "Rog" is away at Camp Devens this summer, but the duties of army life must be endured. John is only fairly well acquainted with his fraternity brothers since becoming a member of the local Congreve Club.

name: Rollins, Gladys Louise
motto: "She doeth little kindnesses which most leave undone, or despise"
description: This describes Gladys, for she has endeared herself to all by her willingness to help. She is a good sport, an earnest student, and just full of college spirit. Since she has been living in a dormitory, we understand that she has been attending the movies somewhat regularly.

name: Romani, Olympia
motto: "A 'Rich' course in Tech. and one in 'Pee-wees'"
description: "Lamb" has a dual personality and those of us who know her best, know least what to expect of her. One of her idiosyncrasies is her neck-tie collection. If you are not already acquainted with it and are about six feet tall, take warning, you are surely on her list for future attack.

name: Rose, Harry Broadbent
motto: This won't make the little Roses heirs to millions
description: When Harry decided to enter the halls of learning, knowing the temptations of a college campus, he made up his mind to commute. However, at the beginning of his Sophomore year, he decided to stop awhile. Since then, due to his sweeping personality, he has spent a few hours every day at Conant Hall. That is, when he is not sharking his E. E. courses.

name: Russell, Charles Henry
description: "Charlie" hails from Winthrop-by-the-Sea, but he isn't wet at all as the town might indicate. "Charlie" has two great vices - women and pennies. We aren't sure which he likes better, but we do know that he spends a lot of time trying to figure how he can keep both. We wonder which of the two is responsible for "Charlie's" return home from that Casque and Casket dance at nine o'clock. In spite of these faults, "Charlie" is well liked by all his acquaintances, and we'll always remember his numerous good qualities.

name: St. Clair, Elgar Lincoln
description: Owner of the radio station I - CKK. Elgar is now working on a problem which has baffled physicists, and which he expects to solve by his knowledge of the "how" of radio. This year his bashfulness has vanished, and we fear he has but little time for research in that direction. When at home he rides in nothing less than a Chrysler, but here - well any old Ford will do, if it's the right coupe.

name: St. Clair, Roger Couch
description: He came to this college with the class of 1927 and took a course in E. E. After five terms of this, he suddenly disappeared and when next heard of was an I. E. in the Class of 1928. He is quite a man with the women, having been seen with many of our fair Co-eds and even the stenogs. He is also very versatile, being both a songster and a bus driver.

name: Sargent, Eben Rolfe
motto: This wop from Penacook, the wide place in the road above Concord, is now a real forester, and the pride of that department. He lost his amateur standing when he practically chopped off a leg just before the Mil. Art hike. His greatest honor came at Forestry camp at Hegan Pond, when he posed as September Morn for a photographic study. He has a decided leaning toward music, and his ability with the harmonica is remarkable.

name: Sargent, Francis Albert
motto: 'Sarge' has several ambitions. In the first place, he wants to be a pugilist. He has surely succeeded there. Recently he decided that he wanted to teach in Leslie School. We haven't heard of his progress in that direction. Now, strangest of all, he seems very interested in 'Marrying Mary.' One of the boys told us that this was a musical comedy. It may be so, but if it is he'll never see it in Lebanon.

name: Sargent, Harrison Erastus
motto: 'Sarg' was handicapped during his sojourn at the University not by the lack of brain matter as some of us were, but by the illness that kept him out of school the entire spring term of our Freshman year. He sure showed us what could be done with Calculus when he succeeded in passing off two terms' work in one. As a Mechanical Engineer he is bound to be a success, and we all wish him the best of luck.

name: Sargent, Malcolm Benjamin
motto: 'Sarge' is one of the sharks of the class. He is also one of the star members of the Rifle Team. 'Sarge' is always ready for a good time. He is one of those few students who are always ready to do more than their share of any job.

name: Sawyer, Charles Murray
description: We have a fine story to tell, but lack of space prohibits it. Murray, whom we might affectionately call by many other pet names, transferred from Dartmouth in early youth. He is a very good mixer, which capacity did not come to him naturally, but as he tells us, was coddled along by him in his various associations. An ambitious motion picture concern wanted him to play the hero in a "Don Marquis" but modestly he refused. His associates have made him what he is today. Need we say more?

name: Schurman, Charles Artis
description: And when the dawn of 1924 came, there stood a bright-eyed chap and they called him "Chick" because he was always peeping. And so began time. A dabbler in the fine arts, an excellent critic, athlete of the first water, but still to the old port city. Glance at the photograph — those eyes, that nose, and especially that halo of hair — character!

name: Scribner, Bernard Morrill
description: "Scrib" is one of the best fellows on the campus. He never blows his own trumpet, but says it with a violin. His one big handicap is his retiring spirit, but when it comes to Alpha Psi Delta Girls he runs against time only — Neck and neck for one lap. Ask Smith.

name: Sebra, Louis Joseph
description: When "Lou" first got off the train at Durham, he dived into DeMeritt and he's stayed there ever since, designing houses for Arts and Crafters to live in. Occasionally he sallies out for a raid on Smith Hall or to greet a flivver coupe from the home town. During cold weather here, (September to June) he writes letters to California; to compare temperatures, we suppose.

name: Sheehan, John Francis
motto: Johnnie's what fair co-eds call a 'pretty nice kid,' and although he is planning on a bachelor's career, we advise him that his plans are very fragile. N. H. U. will certainly miss this bright spark (not because his hair is red) when he graduates. That smile is ever present. John's going to be an M. D. but we advise you not to hang out your sign in Nashua.

name: Simpson, Edwin Kershaw Jr.
motto: Ed comes from Tilton, the home of the smooth boys. He is a conscientious fellow, especially when it comes to choosing co-eds. Ed made his choice early in his college life and has been sincere ever since. His smile and cute blue eyes mark him well. 'There's a reason,' and we think that it is not due to grape-nuts, either.

name: Simpson, Leroy Clayton
motto: Zeke seems to be on the run all of the time, but when we realize what he accomplishes during the day we cease to wonder. He works a great deal, keeps his radio going all night, and sharks most of his courses. Still he finds plenty of time to enjoy the scenery from the E. E. Library window.

name: Slocum, Gladys Copeland
motto: A merry heart and sparkling eye
description: What's that! Oh, of course, "Squeak" Slocum! a Lasell transfer whom we wish had been with us the former two years. She's a "whiz" in the Chemistry courses and it seems there cannot be a dance without Gladys there to enjoy herself. Yes, she is a Home Ecker, but we are hoping she will never be a Home Wrecker. She is a loyal friend and a corking good sport—in fact she's just "Squeak," and that's enough.

name: Smith, Lawrence Everett
high school: Lincoln High School, Lincoln, N. H.
motto: Smitty is another one of those students who has thus far resisted the coy glances of the Co-eds. He is a shark. Smitty is an excellent Engineer. He succeeds where others fail. We wish him the best of luck as he goes out into the cold, cruel world.

name: Smith, Royal William
high school: Laconia High School, Laconia, N. H.
motto: YES I can cook. Gosh sakes is that all?!
description: Three years ago the S.S. Guilford sailed into Durham and dropped anchor at Gale Eastman's, with "Smitty" as chief engineer. Although "Dad" sent him here to learn scientific farming, he has been known to give considerable time to the study of certain banks in Exeter, especially river banks. With his long experience in slinging hash and washing dishes, he should make some girl an ideal husband.

name: Soderlund, Winifred Maud
motto: To know her is to love her
description: Even if "Soddie" does uphold Harvard, she has a place in her heart for N. H. She isn't the athletic type; social functions are more suited to her taste. She has made a host of friends by her understanding and sympathy, for she is always ready to smile and applaud a friend in her achievement and sympathize and help her in adversity. One just can't help loving "Soddie" for her even temper and her thoughtfulness.

name: Spiller, Doris Nathalie
high school: Dover High School, Dover, N. H.
motto: Dot used to be the most popular girl in the College of Technology, but now she has joined the Liberal Arts girls. She is a person who never talks unless she has something to say, but friends suspect that there is plenty of conversation when "Towle" calls Dover.

name: Steeves, Muriel Frances
motto: And then I met Steve
description: That girl with stern and determined look could do nothing but succeed. She must have things done on time, and even expected the boys to be, when preparing a debate. She likes to spend her week-ends at home, in Dover, where it is at least quiet. In spite of her studies she finds plenty of time to study human nature.

name: Stephens, Edna Beede
description: Did you ever hear of Wilton? Well, that's where Edna hails from. She wasn't so particular about returning there over the week-end, but now - try to keep her on campus. You see there's a reason - pretty fast one too. That's why Edna is out for track - to keep up with him.

name: Stevens, John Fisher
description: "Steve," otherwise known as the sheik of the Commons, and the most brilliant mathematician in the history of the University, was getting along O. K. until his Junior year. Sad to say, his mind wandered then, and his three great temptations: photography, women and the church ruined his scholastic standing. Seriously, though, "Steve" really expects to become an Electrical Engineer, and if he hits the harder line of life as well as he has hit the line of scholarship, we all know he will be a top-notcher. We do wish he would shave that off, though.

name: Stockwell, Frank Whitten
description: "Pup" inherited the name from his musical brother, but goes in for another type of indoor sport himself. As far as women are concerned, he has followed in his brother's footsteps and no breath of scandal has ever been heard about him. His cheerful grin did much to mitigate 1929's awe of the dread Sphinx.

name: Servilla, Annette Stoddard
motto:
Let me silent be
For silence is the speech of love
The music of the spheres above
description: "Billie" is so very feminine that she just can't help being a man-hater. And man-hater she is in every sense of the word. She is the kind who knows what to wear and how to act on every occasion. There is no doubt but that the report of her charm and personality will precede her in winning many friends and appreciations.

name: Story, Dorothy
motto: A good heart is better than all the heads in the world
description: "Dot" has both the heart and the head. In spite of her oft-repeated protest that she is stupid, her marks don't bear her out. She knows more about French than most of us do about English. "Dot" is always busy, but her time is scientifically divided between studying, eating, sleeping, and recreation.

name: Sullivan, Mary Louise
description: "Wasn't that terrible, huh?" comes from the hall after one of Dr. Rudd's "psy" exams. "Oh! I inoculated the wrong bug into that tube," we hear in bacteriology. Louise can take the edge off any instructor's grouch, but she knows how to study, too. Petite and always optimistic, she is the "spice of life" to all her classes.

name: Sullivan, Mary Margaret
motto: Better late than never.
description: Mary's motto: 'Better late than never.' She even worked it on a Physics prof - and got away with it. Although she's not "Doc" Slobin's grinding mark puller, she'll make his best teacher. "Sully's" a "happy-go-lucky" pal to everyone and we wish her the best o' luck in whatever she undertakes.

name: Swasey, Muriel Elin
motto: Much water goeth by the mill that the miller knoweth not of.
description: Muriel loves to "put one over" on us. We've heard that "Swarzie" has another nickname too, but we won't tell what it is. She is an ardent football fan and seems to enjoy Freshman games even more than Varsity. We wonder why! However Muriel is a good athlete herself, and it is only natural that "birds of a feather flock together."

We all know she will be a scholar yet, in spite of our efforts. Never mind "Pug," a stubborn spirit frequently wins out—ask your roommate.

name: Taylor, Byron Pineo
description: "By" commutes to Wheaton every week-end. We don't know just why, but the rumor is that he has certain interests there. "By" came to college to study, but he says that "life is too short to let his studying interfere with his week-end visits."

name: Thompson, Marjorie Lucille
motto: If there were dreams to sell, do I not know full well what I would buy?
description: "Marge's" dreams! She also has firm convictions and the strength of them. "Marge" is a peach of a girl and her versatility is shown by her ability to shark certain courses, play hockey, and record the happenings of her illustrious class. And as for loyalty—ask Windy.

name: Toolin, Paul Vincent
high school: LEOMINSTER HIGH SCHOOL AND VERMONT ACADEMY
motto: Get Hot
description: Paul Vincent "Get Hot" Toolin blew into town on a stormy day, and the town has been in an uproar ever since. He burned up the cinders to such an extent his sophomore year that it became necessary to resurface the track, after he broke all college records in the high and low hurdles, but his greatest delight is burning up the town in general. The versatile "Kid" is a mean stepper when it comes to "tripping the light fantastic" and although not wild about women, he knows them all.

name: Torrey, Margaret Bean
description: "Peg" is a hustler and she's as clever on the hockey field as she is in Dean French's "Soci" classes—and that's saying something. Just at present she is interested in the mails—particularly a male in Chatham. Ten years from now she will be a social service worker in Durham running playgrounds and Pancake breakfasts. "Peg" is a firm upholder of the theory that if studies interfere with pleasure, cut out studies.

name: Towne, Sumner Andrew
description: Out of the crowded city of Dedham, to the fields and flowers of Durham came this aspiring young man to the noble class of 1928. "Towne" immediately became attracted by the Band and by the co-eds. As a consequence, he has been leading a dual existence ever since.

name: Trudell, Edmund Albert
description: "Ed" was restless when he first struck Durham, but after discovering the Geology department, he settled down to enjoy college until the Rock courses ran out. Vacations spent on balky trucks have made him appreciate Durham, and qualified him diplomatically for Casque and Casket. "Ed" is an "Eco" major—in theory at least.

name: True, Russell Marston
motto: Rus hails from the great city of Hampton, and is not afraid to tell the world about it. We understand that he has the police force there all trained so that when they see his tin 'Liz' coming they set aside all thoughts of duty and make a low bow. By the way, said 'Liz' has set some enviable records as a week-end bus to and from Hampton. If you want to get 'Rus'' goat, tell him that M. E. is a graft course, or that all Engineering profs. are perfect. He has a serious and solemn look, but it belies his real nature. The Engineer who beats 'Rus' to the goal of success will have to step on the gas and advance his spark to the limit.

name: Turschmann, Carl Emil
motto: And when I have gone forth into the world, I shall accomplish great things.
description: One will come to Carl, with seven or nine kid sisters to support, when some day the Salmon Falls river is harnessed. Carl surely will be to blame. We have never seen him with a woman, yet—"even a queen has her moments."

name: Valakis, John Demosthenes
motto: Let me die drunk with the Dreamer's Wine.
description: "Val" has dreamt of many imaginary Utopias and if he had his own way, he would change the whole universe. What has been Columbia's loss is our gain. He has been with us only a year, but his amusing personality has won many friends for him. We feel mighty proud to have him as our classmate. Good luck, "Val," and may your success continue forever.

name: Vennard, Harold Dame
motto: Hal is just about the smoothest number. Too bad he is so darned bashful. He is the song-bird of the class, in fact his song brings tears to the eyes of all who hear it, it is so effective you know. 'Hal' has nothing to do with halitosis or Listerine. His favorite song is 'In the Sweet Bye and Bye.'

name: Roger, J. Vincent
description: "Twenty" is one of the few who came to the campus with a purpose, stays on the campus with a purpose, and will probably leave the campus with the purpose more firmly in mind than ever. He has always worked conscientiously and has never strayed from his purpose. We are sure that he will accomplish his purpose and when he does, and not until then, will we disclose her name.

name: Leona, Waite
description: Durham is alright for Leona during the week, but when it comes Saturday, on to Manchester! She has acquired considerable skill in "thinking" things and guessing them correctly, especially in bacteriology; and have you seen her medal she won in Springfield? Really, it's quite the cheese!

name: Maurice, Gilbert Waling
description: "Muggsy" made a name for himself with a spade. He slings a mean line, for he can even revive the Parrot and then live with a little scratch in his throat. He is good at smoothing over the rough places and patching up broken hearts. Among his accomplishments, he numbers dramatic ability, and he can build an ice palace on occasion.

name: Russell, Gould Wallace
description: This blue-eyed Engineer hails from the city of Keene. "Rus" claims to have had a fine time while in high school, and says he is having a still better time in college. Just follow him to Dover some evening. Wentworth Hospital must have more attractions than we know of. Ask "Rus."

name: Ward, Edward Hugh
motto: Ned drifted up here from Wakefield, Massachusetts, with a line that was only partly developed—rusty in spots. Three years later he made the Varsity Debating Team, thereby establishing his reputation in the 'glib and oily.' His remarkable oratorical ability made a distinct impression upon a certain Co-ed some time ago and now 'Ned' moves in a sort of daze. 'Ned' has been pronounced a good scout by his friends, and this alone is enough to erase any doubt in our minds regarding his character.

name: Warren, Arlin Brown
motto: Abe and his faithful pipe may be seen plodding to class together every morning. He spends his week-ends in Portsmouth and returns to live on memories. The next week he goes back to that town for more inspiration. Aside from that one failing, he is always ready to give anyone a helping hand, and we all wish him the best of success in life.

name: Warren, Ruth Evangeline
motto: Have you heard of Ruth? Well, you have probably heard her voice anyway. Since a sad episode during her Freshman year, Ruthie has turned to the Glee Club for consolation. Yet we wonder if she has forgotten or if she would be happy just to hear that familiar voice say: 'Hello,' again.

name: Watson, Alice Louise
motto: Alice is our best chemist, and quite a student. Being a chemist does not detract from her ability to make fudge, as all 'chemickers' will admit. Since the advent of Alice, times have changed, also a certain 'chemicker,' but—the importance of Alice in a research lab is still an uncertain quantity.

name: Watts, Frank Emil
motto: A violet by a mossy stone
clubs: Competitive Drill Winner R. O. T. C. (1)
description: His speed has earned him a place on the track team, and such a fast stepper should not hide himself away from all social activities. For a short time we thought that he was "surely" gone, but "along came Ruth" and "Emo" decided that he would hibernate.

name: Webb, George Daland
description: This blushing blond from Marlboro first surprised us by his appearance in the Glee Club. Why he did not continue is for some one else to guess. "Spike" has nailed a job on the "New Hampshire" staff and prints all the news that seems to fit. The rest of his time is taken up by a course in Congreve, a class which he seldom cuts.

name: Weeks, George Newton
description: A terrible example of what fast life is doing to the youth of America. "Newt" can throw off the past and disregard the call of that old life. The night life of Greenland.

name: Weeks, Norman Stephen
description: Hello Mexico! Norman started out as a Liberal Arts student, but lapses last winter in the "Aggie" buildings made his course uncertain. However Norman, in your new role of Captain of the 1927 Harriers, you should be able to get anything you go after-if it's a long distance race. "Weeksie" used to believe in co-education, but since he had this trouble about changing his course his attitude has been slightly revised.

name: Weinbeck, Alice Emily
description: Quarter past the hour; the door opens; and in walks Alice, smiling sweetly. Tardiness—it's just one of her idiosyncrasies. She's taking architecture this year, and specializing in "Doors." She even takes instruction trips to Hampton Beach week-ends when the weather permits.

name: Wellman, Eleanor Blanch
description: Eleanor does not look especially timid, but her courage completely fails her in the presence of mice or at the thought of the world's suddenly ending. In spite of the distance she lives from the campus, Eleanor is present when anything is going on; perhaps because the "Lord" leads her safely home from every social function. Eleanor's chief accomplishments are wielding the mixing spoon and the hockey stick.

name: Westgate, Warren Adelbert
description: Warren is one of the few members of our class who has already joined the ranks of the benedicts, but after seeing his choice we do not blame him in the least. He is on the right road to success if hard work has anything to do with it, and we know that he is successful as a student.

name: Weston, Stewart Norton
motto: "Come fill the cup and in the fire of spring Your winter garment of repentance fling"
description: "Stewie" seems destined to have a fashion column in Vanity Fair on "what the well-dressed man will wear." We rather guess that such a neat appearance results from continual "riding" to and from Manchester for quite an obvious reason. Now we understand why "ladies prefer blonds." This boy's ability in the classroom is surpassed only by his clever ski jumping which earned him national fame as a freshman at New Hampshire.

name: Wheeler, Evelyn Mason
motto: New London has its charms
description: We all know "Ev" for her good nature. Who could help but like her with her winning ways? How many girls owe their wavy hair to "Evie"? We all envy her, especially when she steps out in the "blue roadster" and—? "Ev," you'll break their hearts yet.

name: Wheeler, Gordon Emery
clubs: The Red Ramblers of U. N. H.
description: Gordon Wheeler is a lover of good music. He can toot a sax very well himself. Gordon believes that travel broadens a man. His work with the "Red Ramblers" last summer afforded him a fine chance to see the world. His ambition is to write a book on "The Psychology of Thievery." That probably is an explanation of the fact that he keeps his tooth-paste locked up.

name: Whittemore, John Kenneth
description: "Whit" managed to keep away from the women during his first two years of college. Then he got a summer job and since his return has taken to letter-writing. Oh yes!—On one dark night he was found to constitute the lesser half of a crowd in the bleachers,—at a time when no ball game was going on, either. No one is able to decide whether the change is for better or for worse.

name: Whitten, Lionel Peterson
description: No doubt "Whit" was responsible for the installation of the Dover bus line. Yet it is beyond us how such an undersized parcel of manhood causes such a riot in Dover social circles. Lionel's judgment of figures has not only qualified him for membership in the honorary mathematical society, but has also earned him the title of "Fix-it-up" Whitten. Size will never hinder "Whit's" making a name for himself in later life.

name: Wilkinson, Randolph Hyde
description: "Senator" Wilkinson from Lyme is best known to us as one of the leading candidates for First Mayor of Durham. His stirring soap-box speeches have thrilled many a co-ed.

name: Williams, Donald Herbert
description: "Don's" greatest accomplishment is his ability at bridge. He has been known to double when the opposition made a grand slam in no trumps.
text: "Don's" greatest accomplishment is his ability at bridge. He has been known to double when the opposition made a grand slam in no trumps.

name: Wilson, Doris Standley
motto: "Worcester, that's the place I long for That's where I want to be"
description: Space prevents from enumerating all her qualities; let it suffice to say we consider "Dorrie": W-insome I—ndividual L—ovable S—incere O—riginal N—aive

name: Woods, Carolyn Elizabeth
description: "Cal" is an Eppinite, she wants you to know. "Cal" can very aptly do so for she is one of our University Debaters and knows how to "sling a line." But we're grateful to old Epping just the same. We'd miss her gentle cry: "Oh, Hazie, hurry up!"

name: Wright, Esther Alice
motto: Bubble, bubble, flows the stream
clubs: Member of Joint Open Forum Committee (3)
description: So goes it all day long! She even sometimes talks in her sleep. She may some day divulge the reason for her song—"Why don't I get a letter"?—when she gets one every day—sometimes. She intends to teach English—she practices on her friends!

name: Wright, Ruth Elizabeth
motto: No sooner met but they looked, No sooner looked but they loved
description: But who can blame 'em? Looking at "Ruthie," we wonder that the whole college isn't at her feet. And can she dance? A dance just isn't without her. However, Ruth shines just as brightly in her classes and on the athletic field as she does at "entertaining." "Ruthie's" stellar qualifications combined with her curly hair and twinkling eyes will surely make a place for her in her chosen profession of teaching French in a young ladies' finishing school.

name: Dane, William Arthur
description: The renowned city of witchcraft never makes idle boasts. Behold this blond son, successor to Apollo and second to none in sartorial elegance. Long years ago he aspired to become a member of the Medical profession, but all his anatomical pursuits were extended in other directions and he graduated Cum Laude from Tufts Medical College. After long years of collegiate experimentation "Bill" proved that he had the stuff by his election to the football captaincy.
